data.title: Cycle Pharmaceuticals acquiring Applied Therapeutics
data.description:
• Applied Therapeutics (APLT), a microcap focused on rare diseases, has agreed to be acquired by privately held Cycle Pharmaceuticals. 
  • Terms call for Cycle to acquire all outstanding Applied shares for $0.088 per share in cash at closing plus one non-transferable contingent value right. CVRs totaling up to $0.40 are based on the approval of new products and achieving at least $200M in annual sales of a product. 
  • Cycle's pipeline is focused on rare genetic disorders, immunology, neurology, and oncology.
  • In October, Cycle launched its first oncology product, Phyrago (dasatinib), a bioequivalent version of Sprycel for leukemias that allows patients to take proton pump inhibitor or H2 receptor antagonist drugs concomitantly to help manage gastrointestinal side effects.
  • In July, the company launched Harliku (nitisinone) as the first-ever FDA approved treatment for alkaptonuria.
  • Applied's most advanced candidate is govorestat, an aldose reductase inhibitor in phase 3 for galactosemia and sorbitol dehydrogenase deficiency.

data.title: CDC study finds COVID shot significantly reduced ER visits for children
data.description:
A fresh study authored by CDC scientists is throwing water on the argument that children do not need the COVID-19 vaccine.

Investigators examined electronic health records in nine states of emergency department or urgent care visits from Aug. 29, 2024–Sept. 2, 2025 in two groups: children 9 months–4 years old, and children and adolescents aged 5–17 years old.

Results, published in the Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report (MMWR), found the vaccine cut the the risk of ED/UC COVID-related visits by 76% compared to those who didn't get the shot in the first group, and 56% in the second group. 

"These findings suggest that vaccination with a 2024–2025 COVID-19 vaccine dose provided children with additional protection against COVID-19–associated ED/UC encounters compared with no 2024–2025 dose," the authors wrote.

The study noted that the higher rate in younger children might might due to younger children having lower rates of previous SARS-CoV-2 infection.

The authors also noted that infants 6–11 months old during the 2024-25 repiratory virus season had the highest rates of COVID–associated hospitalization of any vaccine–eligible pediatric age group, adding hospitalization rates in this group were higher than all adult age groups other than those aged ≥65 years.

data.title: The Southern Company rises after six straight sessions of decline
data.description:
The Southern Company (SO) snapped six straight sessions of losses on Thursday as its shares settled 0.77% higher at $84.73, after Seeking Alpha analyst  Narek Hovhannisyan upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold.

The company ended in the red in each session between December 3and December 10. It declined by over 4% during the period.

Hovhannisyan’s upgrade came after the company’s better-than-expected third quarter results in October and on expectations of the fourth quarter beating consensus.

“I have a positive outlook on the fourth quarter. The company's revenue structure is changing dramatically, with the energy consumption of data centers coming to the fore, rather than households. And data centers will consume energy in the fourth quarter, thereby pushing the company's sales up… I expect revenue of $6.45-6.50 billion and EPS in the range of $0.55–0.60,” the analyst said.

Overall, Seeking Alpha, as well as Wall Street analysts, have a Hold rating for the stock.

Seeking Alpha’s quant rating also suggests a Hold recommendation for The Southern Company, with a score of 3.17 out of 5. SO has been rated an A for profitability but has been graded D- for momentum.

On a year-to-date basis, the stock has inched up by only 3.39% while in the past year it has risen by 2.16% lagging behind the wider market.

“The current correction in the share price makes it possible to purchase a high-quality company at a reasonable price and with the potential to grow by 10-15% during the year. The data center boom is driving demand for electricity, which is clearly beneficial to Southern and allowed the company to exceed expectations for third-quarter results,” Hovhannisyan wrote in his article.

data.title: Lululemon in charts: U.S. revenue continues to decline while China sales perk up
Inneova Holdings receives Nasdaq notice for minimum bid price non-compliance
data.description:
• INNEOVA Holdings (INEO) on Thursday said it received a Nasdaq notice on December 8 stating the company no longer meets the exchange’s minimum $1 bid price requirement, after its shares traded below that level between October 24 and December 5.
  • The company has until June 8, 2026, to regain compliance. If unsuccessful, it may qualify for an additional 180-day period if it meets other Nasdaq Capital Market listing standards and indicates it plans to resolve the deficiency, potentially through a reverse stock split.
  • Inneova said its shares will continue trading under the ticker INNEOVA during the compliance period.
  • The company is evaluating options but said there is no assurance it will regain compliance.
  • INEO +2.19% after hours to $0.79.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lockheed Martin awarded $104.6M U.S. Navy contract modification
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin (LMT) has been awarded a $104.6M modification to a previously awarded U.S. Navy contract for the production of the Foreign Military Sale portions unit of the Solid-State SPY ASEA Radar. 
  • This contract involves FMS to the government of Canada. 
  • Work will be performed in Moorestown, New Jersey, and is expected to be completed by January 2032. 
  • Naval Sea Systems Command, Washington, D.C., is the contracting activity.

data.title: PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust prices $75M reopening of 2029 notes
data.description:
• PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust (PMT) said on Thursday its subsidiary PennyMac Corp. priced a $75 million reopening of its 8.500% exchangeable senior notes due 2029 in a direct placement.
  • The notes will form part of the same series as the $216.5 million issued in May 2024.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 15, 2025.
  • The notes, guaranteed by PMT, pay interest semiannually and mature on June 1, 2029. They are exchangeable at an initial rate of 63.3332 common shares per $1,000 principal amount, implying an exchange price about 25% above PMT’s December 11 closing share price.
  • PMT expects net proceeds of about $75.5 million, which will be used to repay borrowings under its mortgage servicing rights and advance facilities, repurchase or repay a portion of its 5.500% notes due 2026, and for general corporate purposes.
  • PMT +0.07% after hours to $12.62.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mexico is close to approving Citi's sale of Banamex stake - report
data.description:
Mexico's financial regulators are close to greenlighting billionaire Fernando Chico Pardo's offer to buy a 25% stake in Citigroup's (C) Mexican retail bank Banamex, indicating the deal will close sooner than originally expected, according to a media report on Thursday.

The approval process for the acquisition and Chico Pardo's strategic plan is in final stages, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the process.

When Chico Pardo and members of his family agreed in September to acquire the stake for ~MXN 42B ($2.3B), Citi (C) said the deal was expected to close in H2 2026. Earlier this week, Citi Chief Financial Officer said the transaction would close "very soon. I talked about next year, I can envision it closing even sooner than that."

The sale is part of Citi (C) CEO Jane Fraser's plan to streamline the bank and exit about a dozen retail markets to focus on four money-center regions. 

The completion of the deal paves the way for a listing of Banamex as soon as next year, Bloomberg said.

Chico Pardo is the former CEO of Carlos Slim's Grupo Financiero Inbursa. He's also a major shareholder in Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste, or Asur, the articles said.

data.title: Alpine Banks of Colorado authorizes $5M stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Alpine Banks of Colorado (ALPIB) on Thursday said that its board has approved a stock repurchase program under which Alpine may purchase up to $5 million of its issued and outstanding Class B voting common stock.
  • The repurchase program is expected to begin on or about February 5, 2026, following completion of the company’s previously announced exchange offer for Class B shares.
  • Alpine said the timing and volume of repurchases will depend on market conditions, available funds, regulatory requirements and other factors.
  • The program runs through July 31, 2026, unless ended earlier, and may include open-market, block or private transactions, including trades under Rule 10b-18 and 10b5-1 plans.
  • The company said it is not obligated to repurchase any specific amount of stock and may alter or terminate the program at any time.
  • ALPIB closed -0.0% at $38.0.
  • Source: Press Release
